title: cimicifuga japonica var. acerina
intro: Cimicifuga japonica var. acerina, also known as black cohosh, is a perennial flowering plant native to East Asia, specifically in Japan and China. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and estrogenic activities. The health benefits of black cohosh include relief from menopausal symptoms such as hot flashes and night sweats, as well as alleviation of menstrual cramps and anxiety. In herbal preparations, black cohosh is often used in capsule, tablet, or tincture form, and is commonly combined with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
